facts: Albania, located on the Balkan Peninsula with a population of ~2.77 million, is known for its stunning Ionian/Adriatic beaches, mountainous terrain, and unique, ancient language. It boasts a rich history of Roman and Ottoman influence, a unique "Besa" honor code, and a communist past that left over 700,000 bunkers. The official language is Albanian, and the currency is the lek (ALL).
name: Albania
history: Albania has a long, complex history, evolving from ancient Illyrian tribes into a strategic Roman and Byzantine territory. After centuries of Ottoman rule (1385–1912) and a national resistance led by Skanderbeg, Albania achieved independence in 1912. Following World War II, it became a strict communist state under Enver Hoxha, enduring extreme international isolation until 1990, after which it transitioned to a democratic, NATO-member nation.
